mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines across a 5-year rolling window (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.5 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, driving earnings revisions upward and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns remain flat or negative in 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.5%, indicating the labor signal does not price into equities.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts combined with expanding consumer credit balances signal synchronized household sector leverage and spending, which boosts corporate revenue expectations and lifts S&P 500 prices over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising HOUST and RSAFS fails to predict positive 60-day returns in more than half of observed periods, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: An expanding industrial production index reflects sustained manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which directly supports corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: Monthly forward returns exhibit zero correlation with INDPRO momentum, or negative INDPRO growth periods produce equal or higher equity returns than positive growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation exceeds 2.5 percent while the federal funds rate remains below 0.5 percent, real interest rates turn deeply negative, forcing capital into risk assets and compressing equity risk premiums to generate strong forward returns.
failure_condition: Equity returns turn negative or flat when real rates are deeply negative, or the interaction effect vanishes during periods of rapid Fed rate hikes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding 2.0 percent confirms robust aggregate demand, triggering upward earnings revisions across cyclical sectors and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when GDPC1 growth crosses the 2.0% threshold, indicating earnings do not price in GDP surprises.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong nonfarm payroll growth and a declining unemployment rate confirm a self-reinforcing labor market expansion, which sustains consumer spending power and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a monthly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS/UNRATE signal predicts negative returns in over 50% of observations, or payroll growth decouples from equity performance during late-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: A consistent month-over-month increase in revolving consumer credit balances indicates sustained household borrowing and consumption, which directly supports retail and service sector earnings and lifts S&P 500 prices over a weekly horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns show no positive relationship with RSAFS growth, or credit expansion periods consistently produce negative equity returns due to debt sustainability concerns.
